steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Sentence 2 gives the F-5 Tiger jet as a sleek, aerodynamic example that glides easily. Sentence 3 describes a boxy semitruck that encounters high wind resistance — the opposite aerodynamic outcome. The blank must signal a contrast between two independent examples under the same general principle.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — As a result,: signals cause-and-effect. The semitruck's wind resistance is not caused by the F-5 Tiger's aerodynamics; they are separate examples. ✗
  • Choice B — Specifically,: signals a narrowing to a more detailed instance of the same thing. The semitruck is not a specific version of the F-5 Tiger — it is the opposite case. ✗
  • Choice C — In conclusion,: signals a summary wrapping up an argument. The passage is still building its case with a second example, not concluding. ✗
  • Choice D — On the other hand,: signals a contrasting idea of equal weight, correctly pairing the aerodynamic F-5 Tiger against the non-aerodynamic semitruck. ✓
D is correct.
